Label: intracellular flux
Definition: Net rate at which a metabolic reaction proceeds in nominal direction within a living biological system

Label: fluxome
Definition: The complete set of metabolic fluxes (reaction rates) in a biological system at a given point in time.

Label: fluxomics
Definition: Experimental approaches to determine the rates of metabolic reactions - the metabolic fluxes - within a biological entity.

Label: Phenotype
Definition: A directly observable biochemical characteristic of a living biological system

Label: metabolic pathway
Definition: a series of connected biochemical reactions catalyzed by enzymes

Label: metabolic network
Definition: a set of interconnected pathways of biochemical reactions within living cells

Label: metabolic reaction
Definition: a biochemical reaction within a living organism and is part of the metabolism.

Label: reaction bidirectionality
Definition: A characteristic of a biochemical reaction which proceeds simultaneously in forward and backward direction

Label: reaction reversibility
Definition: The property of metabolic reaction to proceed in forward and backward direction

Label: unidirectional reaction
Definition: A metabolic reaction that solely operates in a single direction, from educts to products

Label: bidirectional reaction
Definition: A metabolic reaction that operates simultaneously in forward and backward direction.

Label: reaction direction
Definition: A net path that a chemical system to reach or maintain a state of equilbrium

Label: forward direction
Definition: the reaction that proceeds from educts to products

Label: backward direction
Definition: the reaction that proceeds from products to educts

Label: equilibrium
Definition: the reaction that the rate of the forward reaction equals the rate of the backward reaction

Label: reactant
Definition: Starting metabolite of a metabolic reaction

Label: product
Definition: Product metabolite of a metabolic reaction

Label: flux map
Definition: Quantitative visualization of reaction fluxes within a metabolic network, typically scaled by flux magnitude and annotated with directionality.

Label: fluxotype
Definition: the specific pattern or distribution of metabolic fluxes within a biological system, such as a cell or organism, under defined conditions.

Label: Stoichiometry-based fluxomics method
Definition: Set of mathemetical approaches for characterizing metabolic states that constrain the possible flux space given a reaction stoichiometry

Label: Constraint-based fluxomics method
Definition: Set of mathematical approaches in which the possible fluxes are constrained by a minimum and maximum range of bounds, and further restrictions such as the reaction stoichiometry or objectives
